growth_records.strain: in situ population, Aalborg West WWTP activated sludge (no pure culture available)
growth_records.agit: shaking (P uptake/release and batch experiments); orbital for R2A growth not specified
growth_records.medium: Aalborg West WWTP activated sludge batch (acetate/glucose amended)
growth_records.cite: Fernando et al. Resolving the individual contribution of key microbial populations to enhanced biological phosphorus removal with Raman–FISH. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0399-7. [PMC6776032]
growth_records.snippet: ~1.0 g P cell−1 h−1 for Ca. Accumulibacter. The cell-specific P-uptake rate for Tetrasphaera was 0.22–0.38*10−13 and 1.49*10−13 g P cell−1 h−1 for Ca. Accumulibacter.
growth_records.notes: Cell-specific ortho-P release rate for Ca. Accumulibacter under anaerobic conditions from activated sludge batch experiment (fresh sludge, acetate 1 mM + glucose 2 mM + casamino acids to 50 mg/L COD, N2-purged, room temp ~22°C, 4.5 h anaerobic, pH 7.0-7.2 throughout). Note: as printed in the article the value reads '~1.0 g P cell-1 h-1' without the 10^-13 exponent shown for the Tetrasphaera figure in the same sentence; given the magnitude of the other three rates (all ×10-13) this is almost certainly also ×10-13 but the exponent appears to be missing/dropped in the source text, so this is flagged low confidence and quoted verbatim.

growth_records.strain: in situ population, Aalborg West WWTP activated sludge (no pure culture available)
growth_records.agit: shaking (P uptake/release and batch experiments); orbital for R2A growth not specified
growth_records.medium: Aalborg West WWTP activated sludge batch (acetate/glucose amended)
growth_records.cite: Fernando et al. Resolving the individual contribution of key microbial populations to enhanced biological phosphorus removal with Raman–FISH. The ISME Journal (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41396-019-0399-7. [PMC6776032]
growth_records.snippet: The cell-specific P-uptake rate for Tetrasphaera was 0.22–0.38*10−13 and 1.49*10−13 g P cell−1 h−1 for Ca. Accumulibacter.
growth_records.notes: Cell-specific ortho-P uptake rate for Ca. Accumulibacter (in situ, no pure culture exists for this taxon) measured during aerobic phase of activated-sludge batch assay (0.5 mM/15.5 mg P/L phosphate added, no carbon source, room temp ~22°C, pH 7.0-7.2, 4.5 h aerobic incubation), calculated from FISH-Raman single-cell poly-P quantification.

growth_records.strain: Clade IIA enrichment (reference genome CAP2UW1); >99% of Clade II, ~95-99% of total Accumulibacter, ~84% of DAPI-stained cells
growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater medium for EBPR sequencing batch reactor (per Garcia Martin et al. 2006)

up.method: reported; anaerobic acetate uptake, linear rate of change normalized to VSS and Clade IIA relative abundance (4.8+/-0.8)

sec.method: reported; anaerobic Mg release (0.8+/-0.02), counter-cation of polyP hydrolysis
reported; anaerobic K release (0.7+/-0.02), counter-cation of polyP hydrolysis
reported; anaerobic PHB SYNTHESIS rate (7.0+/-1) - intracellular storage polymer, NOT excreted; listed as product flux for GEM constraint

growth_records.cite: Oyserman et al. Ancestral genome reconstruction identifies the evolutionary basis for trait acquisition in polyphosphate accumulating bacteria. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.67. [PMC5148189]
growth_records.snippet: Anaerobic acetate uptake was measured at a rate of 4.8+/-0.8 C-mmol (gVSS-h)-1. Anaerobic PHB synthesis was higher than acetate uptake rates and aerobic PHB degradation (7.0+/-1 and 3.4+/-0.5 C-mmol (gVSS-h)-1...uptake and release of P (2.4
growth_records.notes: Kinetic/stoichiometric characterization of an Accumulibacter Clade IIA enrichment in an SBR under cyclic anaerobic-feast/aerobic-famine EBPR operation. This record = ANAEROBIC FEAST phase. Rates are verbatim reported values, calculated by the authors as linear rates of change of each analyte normalized to VSS (volatile suspended solids, biomass proxy) and Clade IIA relative abundance. No specific growth rate (mu) reported. Medium not given in-paper (cites Garcia Martin et al. 2006), so composition:[] and stated:false. VSS used instead of gDW. PHB synthesis (7.0) is intracellular storage, included as a product flux; PHB degradation belongs to aerobic phase (separate record). Ca uptake/release and PHV negligible, not reported. Units retained verbatim as C-/P-/Mg-/K-mmol per gVSS per h.

growth_records.strain: Clade IIA enrichment (reference genome CAP2UW1); >99% of Clade II, ~95-99% of total Accumulibacter, ~84% of DAPI-stained cells
growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater medium for EBPR sequencing batch reactor (per Garcia Martin et al. 2006)

up.method: reported; aerobic Mg uptake (0.7+/-0.06), counter-cation of polyP synthesis
reported; aerobic K uptake (0.7+/-0.4), counter-cation of polyP synthesis
reported; aerobic PHB DEGRADATION rate (3.4+/-0.5) - consumption of intracellular storage polymer

growth_records.cite: Oyserman et al. Ancestral genome reconstruction identifies the evolutionary basis for trait acquisition in polyphosphate accumulating bacteria. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.67. [PMC5148189]
growth_records.snippet: the uptake and release of P (2.4+/-0.4 and 2.1+/-0.4 P-mmol (gVSS-h)-1, respectively), Mg (0.7+/-0.06 and 0.8+/-0.02 Mg-mmol (gVSS-h)-1...) K (0.7+/-0.4 and 0.7+/-0.02 K-mmol (gVSS-h)-1...) were relatively stable across both anaerobic and a
growth_records.notes: Same Accumulibacter Clade IIA SBR enrichment; this record = AEROBIC FAMINE phase. Aerobic P/Mg/K uptake and aerobic PHB degradation (3.4 C-mmol/gVSS/h) reported as author-calculated linear rates normalized to VSS x Clade IIA abundance. PHB degradation listed under uptake_rates because the intracellular polymer is consumed. No mu reported. O2 uptake not reported. Medium not given in-paper (cites Garcia Martin et al. 2006). VSS proxy for biomass.
